name: Sourdough Boule
description: A round loaf with a crispy, crackly crust and tangy, chewy interior. The long fermentation develops complex flavors and natural preservatives. San Francisco's unique wild yeasts give it a distinctive taste.

characteristics.crumb: Open, irregular holes with chewy texture

funFact: San Francisco sourdough gets its unique flavor from Lactobacillus sanfranciscensis, a bacteria found only in the Bay Area.

name: French Baguette
description: The iconic long, thin loaf with a golden crust and soft, airy interior. French law dictates it can only contain flour, water, salt, and yeast. Best eaten within hours of baking.
funFact: The baguette's shape was standardized in 1920 to make it easier for workers to break and share without a knife.

name: Focaccia
description: A dimpled Italian flatbread drizzled with olive oil and often topped with herbs, sea salt, or vegetables. The high hydration dough creates a light, airy texture with a crispy bottom.
funFact: Focaccia predates the Roman Empire and was originally cooked on hot stones or ashes.

name: Brioche
description: A rich, buttery bread with a golden crown and pillowy soft interior. The high butter and egg content creates an almost cake-like texture. Perfect for French toast or eaten plain.
funFact: Marie Antoinette's famous 'Let them eat cake' was likely a mistranslation of 'brioche'.

name: Banana Bread
description: A moist, sweet loaf made with overripe bananas. No yeast required - baking soda provides the lift. Often studded with walnuts or chocolate chips. The ultimate comfort bread.
funFact: Banana bread became popular during the Great Depression when resourceful cooks refused to waste overripe bananas.

name: Ciabatta
description: An Italian slipper-shaped bread with a crispy crust and extremely open crumb. The wet dough creates large, irregular holes perfect for soaking up olive oil or sandwich fillings.
funFact: Ciabatta was only invented in 1982 by a baker trying to compete with French baguettes.

name: Naan
description: A soft, pillowy flatbread traditionally cooked in a tandoor oven. The yogurt in the dough creates tenderness, while the high heat produces characteristic charred spots. Often brushed with ghee.
funFact: Naan was once a royal food in the Mughal court, too luxurious for common people.

name: Challah
description: A beautiful braided bread enriched with eggs, giving it a golden color and tender crumb. Traditional for Shabbat and holidays. The braids can have 3 to 12 strands.
funFact: The braids in challah are said to represent love, truth, and justice intertwined.

name: Pita Bread
description: A round pocket bread that puffs up dramatically in the oven, creating a natural pocket. The steam inside separates the layers. Perfect for stuffing with falafel or dipping in hummus.
funFact: Pita is one of the oldest breads, with evidence dating back 14,000 years in Jordan.

name: Dark Rye Sourdough
description: A dense, earthy bread made with rye flour and sourdough starter. The natural acids in sourdough are essential for rye bread structure. Often flavored with caraway seeds.
funFact: In medieval times, rye bread was called 'black bread' and was the staple of peasants.

name: Southern Cornbread
description: A quick bread made with cornmeal, baked in a cast iron skillet for a crispy crust. Southern versions are typically unsweetened and savory. The hot pan creates an irresistible golden bottom.
funFact: The debate over sugar in cornbread divides the American South like nothing else.

name: Soft Pretzel
description: A twisted bread with a dark, glossy crust achieved by dipping in lye solution before baking. The distinctive flavor comes from the Maillard reaction with the alkaline surface.
funFact: The pretzel shape may represent arms crossed in prayer, created by medieval monks.

name: English Muffin
description: A round, flat bread cooked on a griddle rather than baked. Famous for its 'nooks and crannies' that appear when split with a fork. Essential for Eggs Benedict.
funFact: English muffins are actually more popular in America than in England, where they're called 'muffins.'

name: Pain de Mie
description: A fine-crumbed sandwich bread baked in a lidded pan for perfectly square slices. The enriched dough creates a tender, uniform texture ideal for sandwiches and French toast.
funFact: The lidded pan (Pullman pan) was named after the Pullman train cars where this bread was served.

name: Irish Soda Bread
description: A rustic quick bread leavened with baking soda and buttermilk. The acidic buttermilk reacts with the soda to create rise. Traditionally marked with a cross on top.
funFact: The cross on soda bread was originally meant to ward off evil spirits and let the fairies out.
